free_text: Production routes of 107,109Cd radioisotopes via
charged particle induced nuclear reactions
Stacked target techniqe, activation metod and high
resolution gamma spectrometry were used in each cases.
Seven irradiations were performed at different beam
energies, different particles using different target
materials. Details are given in the releveant SUBENT.
Nuclear data were taken from NUDAT.
The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty of
incident beam energy, uncertainty of stopping power of
each foil due to thickness variation and  straggling
effects.
The uncertainty on the cross section values
are estimated in the  standard way following the
recommendations of the ISO-guide as the square root of
the quadratic summation of the independently
contributing parameters.
Uncertainty of time parameters is not included.
Uncertainty on beam current
target thickness
detector efficiency calibration
gamma-ray abundance
statistical uncertainty on count rates
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model code TALYS, ALICE
and EMPIRE.
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
TS.
